steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a short vertical line for the axis of the jar. The jar is short and squat, so the axis line is short too — but it still keeps both sides honest.

steps.title: Block the jar (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a rounded, slightly squat body around the axis — wider in the middle, narrowing at the rim. Add a shallow ellipse along the rim and another at the base.

steps.title: Mark the lid (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a shallow dome or short cylinder above the rim for the lid. Keep it modest — honey jar lids are small caps, not big tops.

steps.title: Plan the dipper (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, lightly sketch a dipper leaning out of the jar at an angle — a thin stick with a small grooved bulb at the bottom end inside the jar, and a small knob at the top end above it. The dipper crosses the rim, so its line passes in front of the jar's mouth.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the jar
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the body of the jar, the front of the rim ellipse, and the lid in confident strokes. Stop the rim line cleanly on each side of where the dipper crosses — leave that gap for now.

steps.title: Ink the dipper (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw the dipper as two parallel lines for its slender stick, the small knob at the top, and the bulb at the bottom. Where the dipper crosses in front of the jar's rim, your dipper line passes through the gap you left — the rim's outline stops on each side.

steps.title: Suggest the grooves (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add three or four short horizontal ticks across the dipper's bulb to suggest the spiral grooves. Don't draw every groove — a few ticks read clearly.
